channel.title: Travel on firebirdテックトーク
channel.description: Recent content in Travel on firebirdテックトーク

item.title: Mt. Takao Day Trip Guide: Access, Routes, Yakuoin Temple & What to Eat
item.description:
Mt. Takao (高尾山, Takaosan) is Tokyo’s closest mountain escape — accessible from Shinjuku in under an hour, yet rich with forest trails, a 1,200-year-old temple, and sweeping views of Mt. Fuji on clear days. It’s consistently ranked among the world’s most-visited mountains.

This guide covers everything you need to plan a day trip: transport, trails, the temple, the summit, and where to eat afterward.

Getting There

By Train (Recommended)

item.title: Mt. Takao in the Mist: Yakuoin Temple, Summit at 599m & Post-Hike Soba
item.description:
July 21, 2020. A Tuesday.

That summer, the world was holding its breath.

Japan’s state of emergency had been lifted, but things still felt strange. On an impulse, I decided to head to Mt. Takao. No agenda. No plan. Just a need to go outside, break a sweat, and climb a mountain.

Mt. Takao sits in the western edge of Tokyo, accessible by Keio Line from Shinjuku in about 50 minutes. It’s the world’s most-visited mountain — and on this foggy summer day, it felt like a different world from the city I’d left behind.

item.title: Matsumoto 2-Day Travel Guide: Castles, Castle Town & Shinshu Food — Everything You Need to Know
item.description:
Matsumoto is one of those rare Japanese cities that packs a remarkable amount into a small area. Two National Treasures (Matsumoto Castle and Old Kaichi School), a castle town with genuine historic streetscapes, a world-class art museum, mountain spring water, and one of Japan’s best regional food cultures — all within walking distance of each other.

Two days is the sweet spot. One day feels rushed; three days gives you room for a side trip to Kamikochi.

item.title: Matsumoto Castle & Castle Town: A 2-Day Trip to Japan's "Deepest" Fortress【June 2024】
item.description:
I told myself, “One visit to Matsumoto Castle is enough.” And yet the next morning, I was walking back toward it.

That pretty much says everything.

In late June 2024, I spent two days in Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. Home to one of Japan’s twelve remaining original castle keeps, Matsumoto Castle — nicknamed “Karasu-jo” (Crow Castle) for its striking black exterior — was at the top of my list.

item.title: Fukui 3-Day Travel Guide: Model Itinerary, Echizen Crab & Budget (2024)
item.description:
Fukui 3-day travel guide

Fukui is no longer a “maybe someday” from Tokyo—about 2h06 on the Hokuriku Shinkansen at fastest. This guide is the spreadsheet brain: access, a 3-day timetable, crab season basics, and a realistic budget. For 179 photos, restaurant emotion, and play-by-play, open the trip report.

item.title: Toslove Tateyama Luana & Kamogawa SeaWorld: Complete Guide — IT Health Insurance Resort, Access, Costs & Orca Show Tips
item.description:
On the Boso Peninsula in Chiba Prefecture, about two hours from Tokyo by car, sits one of the most unusual value propositions in Japanese travel: a hilltop ocean-view resort where IT industry workers can stay for ¥6,600 per person, including a full multi-course kaiseki dinner and breakfast.

This guide covers how to use the Toslove Tateyama Luana IT-Kenpo resort, how to pair it with Kamogawa SeaWorld, and everything practical you need to plan the trip.

item.title: Tateyama & Kamogawa 2-Day Trip: ¥6,600 Kaiseki Dinner at an IT Health Insurance Resort — and the Orca Show That Got Us Soaked
item.description:
A full kaiseki dinner. Fresh seafood sashimi. A view of the Pacific from a hilltop balcony. And all of it, accommodation plus two meals, for ¥6,600 per person.

This was the trip that made me genuinely grateful to work in the IT industry.

In late October 2024, I spent two days in the Tateyama and Kamogawa area of Chiba Prefecture’s Southern Boso Peninsula. The stay was at Toslove Tateyama Luana — a resort run by the Kanto IT Software Health Insurance Society (IT-Kenpo) — followed by the orca show at Kamogawa SeaWorld. Both exceeded expectations.

item.title: Fukui 3-Day Trip: Dinosaur Museum, Eihei-ji, Tojinbo & Echizen Crab (Nov 2024)
item.description:
→ Planning: Fukui 3-day guide · → Japanese: 福井2泊3日の旅

Fukui, November 2024: dinosaurs, Zen, cliffs, and opening-week crab

March 2024: the Hokuriku Shinkansen finally stops at Fukui. I bookmarked the news and landed on 14–16 November, right after the 6 November crab opening, with one clear mission—eat Echizen crab in season—and three big side quests: Fukui Dinosaur Museum, Eihei-ji, and Tojinbo.

item.title: Tokyo Disneyland Complete Guide: Tickets, Crowds, App, and How to Plan Your Day
item.description:
“Is Tokyo Disneyland worth the price?” “How do I avoid the worst lines?” “The app looks complicated—what do I actually need to know?”

These are the questions I had before visiting in October 2024. This guide answers them based on direct experience.

First-person trip report: Tokyo Disneyland Halloween 2024: A Full Day at TDL

Basic Data

Item Details
Address 1-1 Maihama, Urayasu, Chiba Prefecture
Nearest station JR Keiyo Line: Maihama Station (5-min walk to gate)
From Tokyo Station ~15 min on the Keiyo Line
Standard hours 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM (varies; always check the official site)
Closed days Almost never (occasional maintenance closures)
Official site www.tokyodisneyresort.jp

Ticket Prices (2024)

Tokyo Disneyland uses dynamic pricing—the cost of a one-day passport varies by date and demand.

item.title: Tokyo Disneyland Halloween 2024: Buzz Lightyear, Beauty and the Beast, and a Full Day at TDL
item.description:
October 11, 2024. I spent a full day at Tokyo Disneyland (TDL) for the first time in years.

If I’m being honest, I had some hesitation going in. The tickets aren’t cheap, the lines are long, and part of me thought “isn’t this mostly for kids?” But someone invited me, and I figured—why not give it a shot. October is late Halloween season at TDL. That turned out to be the right call.

item.title: Hakone 2-Day Trip: Hiking the Old Tokaido Road and a French Dinner at Hotel Biore
item.description: “If we go to Hakone, I want to walk the old road.” That one line was all it took to set the plan in motion. September 27–28, 2024: one night in Hakone, Kanagawa. Instead of the ropeway or the pirate ship cruise on Lake Ashi, the main attraction for this trip was walking an actual stretch of the Old Tokaido Road—the stone-paved path that has remained almost unchanged since the Edo period.

item.title: Hakone 2-Day Guide: Old Road Hike, Lake Ashi & Onsen (2024)
item.description:
Hakone is one of Japan’s most accessible mountain-and-onsen destinations—just 90 minutes from Shinjuku. But with so many routes, passes, and accommodation options, deciding where to start can be overwhelming. This guide covers everything you need, based on an actual 2-day trip in September 2024.

For the full trip story, see → Hakone 2-Day Trip Report: Hiking the Old Tokaido Road and a French Dinner at Hotel Biore

item.title: Nagoya 2-Day Travel Guide: Port Aquarium, Nagoya Castle & Nagoya Meshi (2024)
item.description: Nagoya is Japan’s fourth-largest city and the hub of the Chubu region—easily reachable from Tokyo (1 hr 40 min by Shinkansen) or Osaka (50 min). Compact enough to cover its main sights in 2 days, but substantial enough to feel like a real destination rather than a stopover. This guide covers everything you need, based on an actual visit in July 2024.

item.title: Nagoya 2-Day Trip: Port Aquarium, Nagoya Castle & Nagoya Meshi (July 2024)
item.description:
Late July 2024, Nagoya in summer heat. The city is notoriously hot and humid in the dog days of summer, but the aquarium air conditioning and the stone corridors of the castle proved excellent counter-programming. A packed 2-day trip covering Nagoya Port Aquarium, Nagoya Castle, and the unavoidable Nagoya meshi food experience.

Quick Facts

  • Dates: July 25–26, 2024 (1 night, 2 days)
  • Location: Nagoya City, Aichi Prefecture
  • Access: ~1 hour 40 min from Tokyo by Shinkansen / ~50 min from Osaka
  • Cost (per person, estimate): Transport + accommodation + food + entry fees → approx. ¥25,000–40,000
  • Weather: Extremely hot (midsummer inland city)

Day 1: Nagoya Port Aquarium

The Aquarium as Summer Strategy

Midsummer Nagoya is merciless. The decision to make the aquarium the first stop was basically a survival choice: air conditioning. But Nagoya Port Aquarium turned out to be genuinely impressive—not just a cool place to hide from the heat.

item.title: Okutama Hiking Guide: Tengu Falls, Aya Falls & Seoto-no-Yu Onsen (Tokyo)
item.description: Okutama has some of Tokyo’s best hiking, and the Chisoku trail (千足コース)—connecting two distinct waterfalls, a mountain ridge, and a valley onsen—is one of the most satisfying single-day routes in the area. This guide covers everything you need, based on an actual hike in October 2023.

item.title: Okutama Hiking Trip: Tengu Falls, Aya Falls & Seoto-no-Yu Onsen (Oct 2023)
item.description: “Two waterfalls, a ridge, and a hot spring.” The plan looked perfect from the start. October 7, 2023: a day hike in the Okutama hills west of Tokyo—starting at Chisoku bus stop, visiting Tengu Falls (天狗滝) and Aya Falls (綾滝), crossing the mountain ridge, and ending at Seoto-no-Yu Spa in the Akikawa valley.

item.title: Mt. Oyama Day Trip: Cable Car, Afuri Shrine & Views Over Sagami Plain
item.description:
日本語版はこちら → 真夏の大山、ケーブルカーと下社参拝記

Heading to Mt. Oyama in the Summer Heat

It was the last Saturday of August 2023. I wanted a day trip somewhere with a proper mountain feel — not too far from Tokyo, manageable solo, yet with a real sense of altitude and history. Mt. Oyama (大山, 1,252m) in Isehara, Kanagawa fit the description perfectly.

item.title: Mt. Oyama Day Trip Guide: Access, Cost, Trails & Packing List
item.description:
日本語版はこちら → 大山登山ガイド：費用・アクセス・コース・持ち物を完全まとめ

Personal trip report → Mt. Oyama Day Trip: Cable Car, Afuri Shrine & Views Over Sagami Plain

Overview

Mt. Oyama (大山, 1,252m) in Isehara, Kanagawa is one of the most accessible day hike destinations from Tokyo. A cable car runs to Afuri Shrine Lower Hall (阿夫利神社下社) at 696m, making it suitable for first-time hikers and families, while experienced hikers can continue to the summit on a proper trail.
